# Documentation Evaluation Criteria

Detailed scoring rubrics for each quality dimension.

## Q1: Groundedness

| Score | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| 5 | All claims directly supported by source; evidence is clear |
| 4 | Nearly all claims supported; trivial gaps only |
| 3 | Most claims supported; some unverifiable or vague statements |
| 2 | Multiple unsupported claims; speculation present |
| 1 | Majority of claims lack source support |

## Q2: Completeness

Required elements by document type:

| Type | Required Elements |
|------|-------------------|
| Overview | Purpose, key components, usage, dependencies |
| Architecture | System overview, components, data flow, tech stack |
| Getting Started | Prerequisites, build, test, IDE setup |
| Service/Component | Purpose, responsibilities, API surface, usage examples |

| Score | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| 5 | All required elements present and thorough |
| 4 | All required elements present; one or two could be deeper |
| 3 | Most required elements present; some gaps |
| 2 | Several required elements missing |
| 1 | Majority of required elements absent |

## Q3: Relevance

| Score | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| 5 | Every section earns its space; no filler |
| 4 | Nearly all content useful; minor filler |
| 3 | Mostly relevant; some off-topic or redundant sections |
| 2 | Significant filler or off-topic content |
| 1 | More filler than substance |

## Q4: Clarity

| Score | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| 5 | Scannable, well-structured, clear language throughout |
| 4 | Well-organized; minor clarity issues |
| 3 | Readable but could be better structured |
| 2 | Hard to follow; poor structure |
| 1 | Confusing; wall of text; no structure |

## Q5: Actionability

| Score | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| 5 | Reader can immediately act on the information; clear next steps |
| 4 | Mostly actionable; minor gaps in guidance |
| 3 | Some actionable content but mostly descriptive |
| 2 | Primarily passive description; little guidance |
| 1 | Pure description; no actionable information |
